issues.title: Locale-specific landing CTA links to non-localized chat path
issues.rationale: The app routes chat under `/[locale]/chat`, but the CTA uses `/chat`. This forces locale re-detection in middleware and can navigate users to the wrong locale (e.g., leaving `/ko` and ending up in `/en/chat`).
issues.recommendation: Build the chat link with the active locale (e.g., `href={`/${locale}/chat`}`) so navigation preserves the current language deterministically.

issues.title: Language switcher drops query parameters during locale change
issues.rationale: `usePathname()` excludes search params, so switching locale strips query state (e.g., context/filter params). This can break behavior tied to URL parameters after language toggle.
issues.recommendation: Preserve search params (and hash if needed) by combining `useSearchParams()` with the new localized pathname before `router.push`.

issues.title: Published destinations API silently truncates list to 30 by default
issues.rationale: The function is documented/used as the source for the destinations listing, but defaults to returning only 30 rows. As data grows, pages that call it without `limit` will never show destinations beyond the first 30, producing incomplete results and pagination over a truncated dataset.
issues.recommendation: Only apply `take` when `params.limit` is explicitly provided (or pass explicit pagination inputs from callers). Do not hard-cap default list retrieval to 30 if full listing is intended.
